page: Microsoft Azure DS11_v2 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the DSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, 14.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core, and 29 GB of local storage. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with support for AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2, ADX, and FMA extensions.

In benchmark testing, the DS11_v2 demonstrates entry-level performance characteristics. Its CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores rank in the bottom 10%, indicating limited processing throughput for compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation and ray tracing. However, memory subsystem performance is relatively stronger; the Passmark memory score reaches the middle 50% average tier, although raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks remain in the bottom 25%. Small model LLM text generation also achieves an average rating, while prompt processing is weak.

From a resource density perspective, the DS11_v2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.0 GB per core, which provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU processing. The primary tradeoff is the contrast between its average memory performance and its weak-to-poor CPU compute capabilities. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, simple text processing, and low-intensity utility workloads rather than high-throughput data processing or parallelized compute tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DS11_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DSv2 family, powered by a dedicated 2-core Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 14.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 7.0 GB per core, alongside 29 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets. Benchmark results indicate entry-level compute capabilities, with CPU performance in the bottom 10% to 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark tests. Conversely, memory performance is more balanced, with the Passmark memory score and small-model LLM text generation speed reaching the middle 50% average tier. This resource profile presents a tradeoff of weak processing power paired with relatively stable memory performance, making the DS11_v2 suitable for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and low-intensity utility workloads.
